_meta.name: Quiddity Innovations — Ecosystem Registry
_meta.description: Single source of truth for all QI projects: ports, services, relationships, integration contracts.
_meta.note: All projects consolidating under QI Hive (C:\QIH). Projects moving to C:\QIP one by one. AutoPDF registered 2026-05-01. | Reconciliation 2026-05-09: backfilled session_log from .docx + git, registered cognibase/mapsnap/autopdf in Brain, added QI_MaiaGradio service. | Audit 2026-05-13: retired C:\UNIVERSAL folder, repointed stale QI_Dashboard log paths, registered QI_Elevate/QI_HiveIngest/QI_KazeConfigAPI in registry, fixed QI_NayaGradio missing log redirection. | Move 2026-05-13: AutoPDF moved from C:\Users\renne\Downloads\AUTOPDF to C:\AutoPDF (standardized path).

module_interface_contract.description: Every QI project MUST implement these endpoints. This is what enables them to be assembled into the unified app.

required_endpoints.purpose: Liveness check. All monitoring, the unified app, and the validator call this.
Identity. Who am I, what version am I.

required_endpoints.response: Full registry entry for this project (from qi_registry.json)
required_endpoints.purpose: Self-description. The unified app uses this to discover capabilities.

module_interface_contract.cross_project_call_rule: All calls to other QI projects MUST have a graceful fallback. A project must be fully functional even if all its dependencies are offline.
module_interface_contract.api_versioning_rule: Adding response fields = OK. Removing or renaming fields = breaking change. Breaking changes require a new versioned endpoint (/v2/...).

rules: Registry is updated BEFORE code is written — never after
Every project runs standalone without any other QI project
Every project honors the module interface contract
Every project follows QI_Standards.md naming and folder conventions
No port is used outside the project's allocated block
CLAUDE.md exists at every project root
Session summary saved as .docx after every significant session

port_strategy.rule: Each project owns a dedicated port BLOCK. No project may use another project's block.

api_blocks.9000-9099: QI Hive family (Brain at 9011, Dashboard at 8600 migrating)

port_strategy.migration_note: Existing ports below were created before this registry. They WORK and should NOT be changed unless there is a specific reason. New services MUST follow the block strategy.

projects.name: FileHQ
projects.description: File intelligence engine — MERGED into Naya (C:\NAYA\filehq\). Original C:\FileHQ marked for deletion.
projects.family_notes: Merging into Naya. FileHQ becomes Naya's file engine. Phase 1 (bridge) complete. Phase 2 (code absorption) pending Renne approval.

projects.name: Maia
projects.description: Multi-channel AI assistant platform (LINE, Telegram, Messenger, Instagram, WhatsApp)

api.notes: Pre-registry — do not change unless migrating

ui.notes: Pre-registry — do not change unless migrating

projects.family_notes: The flagship product. All other projects ultimately serve Maia or learn from it.
exposes_to_ecosystem: LLM chain (MiniMax → GPT → Qwen3 → Gemma3 → DeepSeek → Qwen3:4b)

projects.name: Naya
projects.description: Personal AI assistant for Renne — AI/physics/programming/networking domains + file scanning

api.notes: Pre-registry — do not change unless migrating

ui.notes: Pre-registry — do not change unless migrating

services.name: NayaBot
services.notes: LAN-only. Telegram uses outbound long-polling — no inbound internet access needed. Never expose via Cloudflare.

telegram.notes: Telegram is the ONLY chat interface for Naya. No LINE, no Messenger, no Gradio UI for users.

projects.family_notes: Personal file management AI for Renne. Absorbed FileHQ as file engine module. Telegram is the only chat interface. LAN-only — no Cloudflare tunnel, no internet exposure.

projects.name: NEXUS
projects.description: Neural Exchange and Unified Synthesis — AI orchestration backbone for all QI projects

api.notes: Pre-registry — within safe range, no conflict

ui.notes: Pre-registry — within safe range, no conflict

projects.family_notes: The AI intelligence layer. Not a user-facing product — it serves all other projects. In the unified app, NEXUS becomes the AI engine module that powers everything.
exposes_to_ecosystem: GET /bench/recommend — LLM recommendation by use case [Phase 2]

projects.name: OpenClaw
projects.description: Autonomous AI agent platform — 6 custom agents (Tasuke, Kaze, Sentry, Seiri, Yubin, Koe)

gateway.notes: WSL listener on 0.0.0.0:18789 (TCP), non-HTTP gateway protocol. Reachable from Windows via WSL loopback.

projects.family_notes: Autonomous agent layer. Where Maia responds, OpenClaw acts. Future: Maia routes complex action tasks to OpenClaw. Strong marriage candidate with Maia for agent-assisted conversations.
cancelled_agents: Seiri — CANCELLED 2026-04-05. File management fully replaced by Naya (absorbed FileHQ).

projects.name: MQ
projects.description: Maia Quiddam — autonomous AI social media persona for Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp
projects.status_reason: Awaiting Facebook Page approval + Meta Developer credentials. Entry point exists (Start_MQ.bat -> main.py). Updated 2026-05-11.
projects.family_notes: New project — family role to be determined as it develops. Status 2026-05-13: not started yet (was paused_pending_credentials; corrected to new).

projects.name: EasyFlow
projects.description: Email organization tool — tier-based inbox management with Gmail API + Apps Script automation. Free gift tool for family/friends.

dashboard.notes: Local Flask dashboard — desktop tool, not a server

projects.family_notes: Standalone desktop tool for email management. Not a server-based QI project. Runs locally for end users. Built as a gift for family/friends with full UI-driven workflow.

projects.name: QI-Universal
projects.description: Universal tools and dashboards shared across all QI projects — QI Launcher, cross-project utilities
projects.migration_note: Migrated from C:\UNIVERSAL into C:\QIH on 2026-04-22 — see C:\QIH\docs\UNIVERSAL_MIGRATION_PLAN.md

launcher.notes: QI Launcher — served via python http.server

projects.family_notes: Universal tools layer. Not a product — shared utilities for all QI projects. QI Launcher at localhost:8650 is the single-page dashboard for all project localhost URLs.
exposes_to_ecosystem: GET http://localhost:8650 — QI Launcher (all project links in one page)

projects.name: QI Brain
projects.description: Shared knowledge substrate for the QI ecosystem. SQLite + ChromaDB + 12-tool MCP server.

api.notes: QI Orchestration block. Port 9010 is permanently squatted by Logitech G HUB (lghub_agent.exe) on 127.0.0.1:9010 — moved to 9011 on 2026-05-14.

projects.name: QI Hive
projects.description: Unified agent orchestration and knowledge system — QI Brain + Dashboard + 7 hive agents. The bee colony.

brain_api.notes: QI Brain API. Moved from 9010 to 9011 on 2026-05-14 — port 9010 squatted by Logitech G HUB.

sub_systems.brain: C:\QIH\engine\brain — knowledge substrate (SQLite + ChromaDB + MCP + 12 tools)
sub_systems.hive: C:\QIH\engine\hive — agent system (7 agents, dashboard, skills, tests)

projects.family_notes: The nervous system and operational layer. Absorbs Claude Manager. QI Brain is the hive mind. Agents are the bees.
exposes_to_ecosystem: GET  http://localhost:9011/api/agents — list all hive agents
POST http://localhost:9011/api/log_session — session logging for all projects
POST http://localhost:9011/api/log_decision — decisions for all projects
projects.future_structure: Projects migrate to C:\QIP one by one. C:\QI becomes C:\QIB (business side).

projects.name: AutoPDF
projects.path_migration_note: Migrated 2026-05-13 from C:\Users\renne\Downloads\AUTOPDF to standardized path C:\AutoPDF. AutoPDF is fully portable — reads everything via relative paths, so the move required no code changes.
projects.description: Self-contained PDF toolkit: convert / split / extract / catalog. Local-only, no cloud.

http.notes: Pre-registry — port 6969 chosen independently. Works correctly. Migration to 8700 block recommended only when adopting other ecosystem integrations.

consumes: Bundled engines: Ghostscript, Poppler, Tesseract, Tabula, PDFtk, Liberica JRE
projects.ecosystem_role: Standalone tool. Can be called by Maia / NEXUS / FileHQ in the future to extract fields from user-supplied PDFs.

projects.name: CogniBase
projects.description: Local desktop platform that connects to Hyland OnBase, lifts its data into a vector store + data lake, and lets AI correlate, infer, and report across boundaries OnBase itself can't query. Vendor-neutral LLM router (Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/Ollama/LM Studio/llama.cpp), pluggable source connectors, OnBase-Web-Client-style retrieval UI, templated and ad-hoc reports. First source: OnBase. Future sources: filesystem, email, Jenzabar.

api.notes: Local FastAPI server. Block reserved for MapSnap family (siblings: mapsnap).

projects.family_notes: Sibling of MapSnap (Jenzabar schema browser). Renamed from 'mapsnap_onbase' to 'cognibase' on 2026-05-06 to reflect the broader mission: not just an OnBase mirror but a platform that exceeds OnBase. Status corrected 2026-05-13: pre_poc (was phase_b_core_complete_pilot_ready; renaming reflects actual maturity stage).

projects.name: MapSnap
projects.description: Standalone local schema browser for Jenzabar EX (university ERP). Reads schema.json built from CSV/SQL exports and renders a navigable HTML browser with FK relationships, ERD-confirmed edges, and module groupings. Original product; sibling of mapsnap_onbase.
projects.primary_language: Python (local HTTP server) + static HTML browser

api.notes: Legacy port 9876 pre-registry. Block 8650-8659 reserved for MapSnap family but not in use here. Per registry rule, existing ports stay unless there's a reason to migrate.

projects.family_notes: Sibling of cognibase (same DNA, different scope). MapSnap = passive Jenzabar schema map. cognibase = live OnBase integration. Kept separate intentionally to insulate the stable browser from the integration product's churn.

family_taxonomy.description: Defines how projects will integrate in the future unified QI platform.

tiers.core: The primary product — Maia. All roads lead here.
tiers.backbone: Infrastructure that powers everything — NEXUS. Never user-facing directly.
tiers.sibling: Same DNA, different face. Share engine, personality, config. Can run as one app with feature flags.
tiers.sibling_candidate: Not siblings yet but heading there. Will share code as they mature.
tiers.cousin: Related domain, shared data/events, call each other's APIs. Independent deployment.
tiers.marriage: Two modules that deeply complete each other. Merged feature = stronger than both.

current_relationships.description: Same assistant engine, different personas (K-12 platform vs personal AI). Will share codebase when template engine is complete.
Maia uses NEXUS for multi-AI synthesis and AI news. NEXUS is the AI backbone, Maia is the interface.

Naya (personal AI) + FileHQ (personal files) merger in progress. Phase 1 complete: full bridge (50+ endpoints), Telegram /files commands, enhanced File Manager UI. Phase 2 pending: physical code absorption into C:\NAYA. Naya = brain, FileHQ = engine.
Maia handles conversation, OpenClaw handles autonomous action. Together = AI assistant that can act, not just talk.
NEXUS Judge/Bench will eventually power LLM selection across all QI projects automatically.

unified_app_vision.description: The future QI unified platform — one app, many modules.

modules.role: Core AI dispatch, synthesis, LLM management

shared_infrastructure.database: SQLite per project (maia.db, nexus.db, etc.) — no shared DB today. Future: federated or migrated to PostgreSQL.
shared_infrastructure.llm_chain: Maia owns primary chain. NEXUS owns evaluation. All projects should query NEXUS for LLM recommendations.
shared_infrastructure.auth: None today — all local/internal. Future: shared auth token when unified app is built.
shared_infrastructure.messaging: No internal message bus today. Future: lightweight event bus (Redis Pub/Sub or simple SQLite queue).
shared_infrastructure.cloudflare_tunnel: Maia ONLY uses Cloudflare Tunnel — required for LINE/Telegram/Messenger webhook callbacks. NEXUS, Naya, and all other projects are LAN-only and must NOT be given Cloudflare tunnels without explicit design review.

nssm_services.note: NSSM binary standardized 2026-04-22 to C:\QIH\engine\bin\nssm.exe — all services use this. Service names use the QI_ prefix. Registry below reflects live audit on 2026-05-13.

registered.name: QI_Dashboard
registered.notes: Log paths repointed 2026-05-13 from C:\UNIVERSAL\dashboard\LOGS to C:\QIH\engine\hive\dashboard\LOGS

registered.name: OC-Keepalive-Service
registered.notes: Pre-dates QI_ naming convention — not renamed since it's OpenClaw-internal

nssm_services.naming_rule: New services: QI_<Project><Role> — e.g. QI_NexusAPI, QI_MQBot. Never reuse names. NSSM binary always C:\QIH\engine\bin\nssm.exe.
safety_rules: NEVER kill a port without checking which project owns it — use qi_registry.json
NEVER expose Naya, NEXUS, or OC via Cloudflare tunnel — Maia ONLY
All services share the same Python (C:\1-AI\APPS\PYTHON\python.exe) — a broken pip install affects ALL projects
Stopping MaiaBot kills LINE/Telegram webhooks — external users lose service immediately
NayaBot auto-starts FileHQ engine on port 8200 — stopping Naya also stops FileHQ
NSSM auto-restarts failed services — killing a port may trigger a restart loop
Before any port change: check ALL services in this registry, not just the current project
